title: Pet Urine on Upholstery
timeEstimate: 30 min – 24 hr; low pro threshold (batting saturation)
stainChemistry: Pet urine = urea + uric acid + pheromones + protein (cat = + felinine). Uric acid CRYSTALLIZES on drying — bonds tightly to fiber. Crystals reactivate with humidity, smell returns weeks later. On upholstery, the upholstery code (W / W-S / S / X) determines water-based steps. Cushion batting saturation is the failure mode — never pour. Enzyme cleaner is the chemistry unlock on synthetic upholstery; wool / silk upholstery = different ladder. Mattress saturation = pet-urine-mattress card.
whyThisWorks: Enzyme cleaner digests uric acid crystals at the depth they reached. On upholstery, MEASURED enzyme application (not the carpet 2–3× saturation) avoids batting flood. Cover dwell extends enzyme without re-saturating. Aggressive weighted extraction prevents wick-back. The honest cap: cushion batting saturation = pro low-moisture extraction (not truck-mount).
defaultAssumption: Synthetic upholstery (polyester / microfiber / cotton blend). WOOL / SILK upholstery = pro from minute one (retail pet enzymes digest those fibers). Mattress = pet-urine-mattress card. Pro escalation threshold is LOW — batting saturation has narrow home recovery.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Gloves. Find code tag (W / W-S / S / X). S-only / X = pro for water-based pet urine. UV blacklight in dark — urine glows yellow-green. Tape full extent. Wool / silk upholstery = pro from minute one (retail enzymes digest those fibers).

spottingProtocol.equipment: Cold water, white cloth, dry towel + weight
spottingProtocol.instruction: Press cloth dampened with cold water on the spot. Lift, rotate, press. Cold dilutes uric acid. Weighted dry towel 5 min. Never pour — batting saturation.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Dawn + cold water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dawn cold water on damp cloth. Lifts surface fat / salts so enzyme reaches uric acid. NO OxiClean — alkaline can set protein.

spottingProtocol.agent: Pet Enzyme Cleaner — Measured, Not Saturated Like Carpet
spottingProtocol.equipment: Pet enzyme cleaner (Nature's Miracle / Rocco & Roxie / Anti Icky Poo / Simple Solution); damp towel + plastic wrap
spottingProtocol.instruction: On synthetic upholstery (W/W-S code). Apply MEASURED to urine volume — NOT 2–3× like carpet. Upholstery can't extract excess. Cover with damp towel + plastic wrap, tape edges. Fresh: 30–60 min. Overnight: 12–24 hr.
spottingProtocol.safetyNote: Wool / silk upholstery = pro (retail enzymes digest these fibers). S-only / X code = pro. Don't layer enzymes with bleach / steam / strong alkali.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Uncover. Weighted dry towels 30 min × 2–3 rounds + overnight. Extraction matters more than application on upholstery — wick uric acid + moisture out of batting before they re-bond.

spottingProtocol.agent: Fan-Dry + Smell + Blacklight Check — 1 Round Max
spottingProtocol.instruction: Fan-dry fully. Smell + blacklight check. Still positive = pro low-moisture extraction. Upholstery has lower pro threshold than carpet — repeat home rounds risk batting saturation worse.

steps: 1. Code tag check + UV blacklight + gloves. Wool/silk = pro from minute one.
3. Dawn damp cloth — clear surface for enzyme.
4. Pet enzyme MEASURED (not 2–3× like carpet) + cover dwell 30 min – 24 hr.
6. Fan-dry + smell + blacklight check. ONE round max.
products: Pet enzyme cleaner (Nature's Miracle / Rocco & Roxie / Anti Icky Poo / Simple Solution) — synthetic upholstery only
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold throughout — heat sets protein + deactivates enzyme
warnings: WOOL / SILK upholstery = pro (retail enzymes digest those fibers)
NEVER hot water / steam — sets protein
MEASURED enzyme on upholstery (not 2–3× carpet saturation)
ONE home round on upholstery — low pro threshold
Pad / batting saturation = pro low-moisture (NOT truck-mount)

homeSolutions.agent: Wool / Silk Upholstery? Pro From Minute One.
homeSolutions.instruction: Before anything: is this wool, silk, or velvet upholstery? Retail pet enzyme cleaners (Nature's Miracle, Rocco & Roxie, etc.) all contain protein-digesting enzyme — protein-digesting enzyme digests wool and silk just like it digests urine protein. If your upholstery is wool / silk / velvet, this card doesn't apply — call a pro from minute one. This card is for synthetic upholstery (polyester, microfiber, cotton-poly blends).

Find code tag (under cushion). W / W-S = proceed. S-only = solvent only, water-based pet enzymes mismatched — pro. X = vacuum only, pro. UV blacklight in dark room finds the full extent — urine spreads farther than visible. Tape with painter's tape. Cushion-cover-zip-off + machine-washable = wash cover separately.

Disposable gloves (biohazard). Press a clean white cloth dampened with COLD water on the spot. Lift, rotate, press. Cold dilutes uric acid + prevents protein setting. Never pour — flood = cushion batting saturation.
Mix Dawn in cold water. Damp cloth, press 5 min. Lifts surface fat + salts so enzyme can reach uric acid. NO OxiClean — alkaline can set protein.

homeSolutions.agent: Pet Enzyme — Measured, Not Saturated Like Carpet
homeSolutions.instruction: Pet-specific enzyme cleaner (Nature's Miracle, Rocco & Roxie, Anti Icky Poo, Simple Solution). On UPHOLSTERY: apply MEASURED to urine volume — NOT 2–3× saturation like on carpet. Upholstery can't extract excess like carpet pad can. Apply just enough to reach the uric acid. Synthetic upholstery only.
Lay a damp (not dripping) white towel over the treated area, cover with plastic wrap, tape edges. Keeps enzyme wet during dwell without re-saturating. Fresh: 30–60 min. Overnight accident: 12–24 hr.

Uncover. Heavy dry towels on the spot, weight with books, 30 min. Replace with fresh towels, repeat 2–3 times. Finish with fresh towels overnight. Extraction matters more than application on upholstery — wick uric acid + moisture out of batting.

homeSolutions.agent: Fan-Dry + Verify — ONE Round Max, Then Pro
homeSolutions.instruction: Fan-dry fully. Smell + blacklight check. Smell gone + no fluorescence = win. Still positive = pro low-moisture extraction. Upholstery has a LOWER pro threshold than carpet — second home round risks batting saturation worse than the original. Tell the pro: "Pet urine on [W / W-S / S / X] upholstery, [cat / dog], age [hours / overnight], one home round with measured enzyme + cover dwell + aggressive extraction. No heat, no ammonia. [Smell / blacklight still positive]."

steps: 1. WOOL / SILK / VELVET upholstery = pro (retail enzymes digest those).
2. Code check (W / W-S / S / X). S/X = pro.
3. Gloves + UV blacklight + tape full extent.
5. Dawn damp cloth, 5 min — clears surface.
6. Pet enzyme MEASURED to urine volume (NOT 2–3× like carpet).
7. Cover with damp towel + plastic wrap. Dwell 30 min – 24 hr.
9. Fan-dry + verify. ONE round max — then pro low-moisture extraction.
products: Pet enzyme cleaner (Nature's Miracle / Rocco & Roxie / Anti Icky Poo / Simple Solution) — synthetic upholstery only
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop after one home round — pro low-moisture extraction. Repeat home rounds risk batting saturation worse. Wool / silk upholstery = pro from minute one.
warnings: WOOL / SILK / VELVET = pro from minute one
NEVER hot water / steam — sets protein, kills enzymes
MEASURED enzyme on upholstery — not 2–3× carpet saturation
ONE home round — low pro threshold
Aggressive extraction matters more than aggressive chemistry

neverDo: Never on wool / silk / velvet upholstery (retail enzymes digest fibers)
Never repeat home rounds beyond 1 — batting saturation
fiberSensitivities: Synthetic upholstery (polyester / microfiber / cotton-poly): tolerates full enzyme protocol
Wool / silk / velvet: protein-digesting enzyme digests fibers — pro lane

materialWarnings: Wool / silk / velvet upholstery = pro from minute one
ONE home round — low pro threshold

escalation.when: Wool / silk / velvet upholstery (always), S-only / X code, smell or blacklight positive after one home round, recurring same-spot accidents, cushion batting saturation suspected, cat-urine persistent mercaptan smell.
escalation.whatToTell: "Pet urine on [W / W-S / S / X] upholstery, fabric [polyester / microfiber / cotton blend / wool / silk], [cat / dog], age [hours / overnight], one home round at home with cold + Dawn + measured pet enzyme + cover dwell + aggressive extraction. No heat, no ammonia, no OxiClean. Need low-moisture extraction (NOT truck-mount)."
escalation.specialistType: professional upholstery cleaner with low-moisture extraction (NOT truck-mount — too much water for upholstery)

contextQuestions: Upholstery code — W / W-S / S / X?
Fabric — polyester / microfiber / cotton blend / wool / silk / velvet?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Wool / silk / velvet = pro from minute one. Microfiber often contains acetate-sensitive fibers (acetone-bans don't apply here, but still — handle gently). Recurring same-spot = batting almost certainly contaminated. Cat urine = harder due to felinine. For mattress, see pet-urine-mattress card.
